habits.productivity: Self-taught programmer, online courses (CodeAcademy, Carnegie Mellon CS Academy), problem-focused mindset
habits.hobbies: Programming, debate, piano, essay writing, reading (economics, politics, AI, business)

personality.traits: Problem-solver, curious, ambitious, leadership-oriented, formerly introverted
personality.humor: also stop asking me for permission to update my profile and who we are
personality.communication: Blunt, direct, expects attention to detail
personality.energy: Introverted but confident in leadership roles, prefers deep 1-on-1 conversations

identity.name: Moise Gasana
identity.background: Rwandan student studying Computer Science and Economics at Brown University. Raised in Kigali, attended Agahozo Shalom Youth Village and Bridge2Rwanda.

values.coreValues: Problem-solving, community impact, innovation, leadership, accountability
values.priorities: Education, creating solutions for Rwanda, advancing CS and tech skills
values.beliefs: Youth can drive change in Africa, technology enables solutions, human-centered leadership
values.dealbreakers: Gender discrimination, lack of accountability, inefficiency

opinions.hotTakes: Rwanda and Africa need innovative solutions in all sectors, youth must lead change, technology is the key to development
opinions.technology: Programming is creativity and problem-solving, computers enable access to infinite possibilities, AI and tech are essential for Africa's future
opinions.life: Community and human connection are essential, problems exist everywhere and demand solutions, impossibilities are meant to be challenged

dreams.longTerm: Create technological solutions for Rwanda and Africa, inspire youth-driven change
dreams.legacy: Generation of young Africans creating solutions for their societies, youth empowerment in Rwanda
dreams.wildDream: Transform Rwanda and Africa through technology and innovation, be a living example of youth-driven change
dreams.shortTerm: Excel at Brown University, build innovative solutions for Rwanda

lifeStory.struggles: Early childhood loneliness, absent father, overworked mother, limited access to technology in early education
lifeStory.turningPoints: Step-siblings arrival (age 9), joining Agahozo Shalom Youth Village, becoming Student Government President, winning debate championships
lifeStory.achievements: 1st place Rwanda National Science Fair, 2x Kigali Debate League winner, 3rd globally Carnegie Mellon CS Academy, Silver Medal Queen's Commonwealth Essay, Student Government President
lifeStory.childhood: Raised by single mother in Kigali, Rwanda. Lost father at age 2. Experienced loneliness, found escape in movies and technology.
lifeStory.currentChapter: Computer Science and Economics student at Brown University (Fall 2021 cohort), focused on creating solutions for Rwanda and Africa
